purpose: Extend the deterministic repair runtime fixup with explicit UI/API/UX, policy and runtime support for selecting which worker/backend is used and where that worker actually executes. This covers fixed assignment and automatic policy-ranked selection for OpenCode, native Ananta Worker, Hermes, remote workers, local Docker/WSL runtimes and other execution targets.
merge_instruction: When editing todo.deterministic-repair-runtime-fixup.json directly, merge this extension as DRR-M10 with tasks DRR-T046 through DRR-T056, update critical_path_tasks, minimum_safe_slice, definition_of_done_global and tasks_status_summary accordingly.
why_extension_file_exists: The base todo.deterministic-repair-runtime-fixup.json is large. This extension is intentionally separate to avoid unsafe overwrite while preserving all existing detailed tasks.
global_principles: Worker selection is a Hub policy decision, not a prompt instruction.
Runtime target selection is separate from worker kind selection.
UI may request fixed assignment or automatic policy-ranked selection, but Hub must validate it before execution.
Every actual selected worker and runtime target must be persisted in repair plan, execution result, trace and audit.
Automatic selection must expose rejected candidates and reasons, so operators can understand why OpenCode/native/Hermes/remote worker was selected or rejected.
Fixed assignment must fail closed when the selected worker/runtime cannot satisfy capability, safety, context, approval or environment constraints.
Cloud/external worker selection must respect context sensitivity and cloud_allowed policy before any context is sent.

tasks_to_add.title: Define WorkerSelectionPolicy schema for deterministic repair
tasks_to_add.problem: Deterministic repair currently needs explicit control over whether the Hub uses native Ananta Worker, OpenCode, Hermes, ShellGPT, remote workers or another worker. This must be structured policy, not a UI text preference.
implementation_notes: WorkerSelectionPolicy added in worker/core/runtime_target.py
WorkerKind, WorkerSelectionMode and FallbackPolicy enums added
fixed, automatic and policy_ranked modes supported
fixed mode requires fixed_worker_id or fixed_worker_kind
automatic mode rejects fixed_worker_id
allowed/denied worker kinds validated
required/forbidden capabilities normalized and conflict-checked
legacy preferred_backend mapping added in agent/services/worker_selection_policy_service.py
JSON schema added at schemas/worker/worker_selection_policy.v1.json
tests added in tests/test_worker_selection_policy.py
acceptance_criteria: WorkerSelectionPolicy includes mode, fixed_worker_id, allowed_worker_kinds, denied_worker_kinds, required_capabilities, forbidden_capabilities, prefer_local, allow_cloud, allow_external_workers, require_code_context, risk_profile, fallback_policy and selection_reason_required.
Valid worker kinds include native_ananta_worker, opencode, hermes, shellgpt, remote_worker, custom_worker and disabled_placeholder or equivalent project-specific enum.
mode='fixed' requires fixed_worker_id or fixed_worker_kind.
mode='automatic' forbids fixed_worker_id unless explicit fallback pinning is supported.
allow_cloud=false rejects cloud/external worker candidates before context is sent.
Unknown worker kind is rejected by schema or normalized to denied with reason_code='unknown_worker_kind'.
WorkerSelectionPolicy can be embedded in RepairProcedureExecutionPlan and ExecutionEnvelope metadata.
test_requirements: Schema tests for fixed, automatic and policy_ranked modes.
Negative tests for unknown worker kind, fixed mode without fixed worker, cloud worker with allow_cloud=false.
anti_patterns: Do not store selected worker only as free-text preferred_backend.
Do not let user prompt decide worker kind.
Do not silently fall back from fixed worker to another worker.

tasks_to_add.title: Define WorkerRuntimeTarget schema
tasks_to_add.problem: Choosing OpenCode or native worker is not enough. The system must know where the worker actually runs: local process, Docker container, WSL, remote URL, dedicated machine, CI sandbox or cloud runtime.
implementation_notes: WorkerRuntimeTarget added in worker/core/runtime_target.py
WorkerRuntimeKind, RuntimeDataBoundary, RuntimeHealthState and SecretAccessPolicy enums added

Runtime target fields include runtime_target_id, runtime_kind, location, endpoint_ref, workspace_scope, os_family, containerized, network_zone, allowed_capabilities, denied_capabilities, available_tools, max_parallel_tasks, resource_limits, data_boundary, secret_access_policy, health_state and validation_errors
mutation-capable runtime requires workspace_scope
secret-capable runtime requires explicit non-deny secret policy
local/cloud/external classification helpers added
WorkerRuntimeTargetService added with local_process_default and docker_default helpers
JSON schema added at schemas/worker/worker_runtime_target.v1.json
tests added in tests/test_worker_runtime_target.py
acceptance_criteria: WorkerRuntimeTarget includes runtime_target_id, runtime_kind, location, endpoint_ref, workspace_scope, os_family, containerized, network_zone, allowed_capabilities, denied_capabilities, available_tools, max_parallel_tasks, resource_limits, data_boundary, secret_access_policy, health_state and validation_errors.
Valid runtime_kind includes local_process, docker_container, docker_compose_service, wsl, remote_http_worker, remote_ssh_worker, ci_sandbox, cloud_worker and custom.
Runtime target declares whether it can access project files, Docker socket, network, secrets, shell and service control.
Runtime target validation rejects mutation-capable repair if workspace_scope is missing.
Runtime target validation rejects secret access unless policy explicitly grants it.
Runtime target is referenced by RepairProcedureExecutionPlan, WorkerJobDB and RepairExecutionResult.
test_requirements: Schema tests for local Docker, WSL, remote HTTP and cloud worker targets.
Negative tests for mutation runtime without workspace scope and secret access without policy.
anti_patterns: Do not infer runtime safety from URL string only.
Do not assume all OpenCode workers run locally.
Do not use agent.url alone as proof of runtime environment.

tasks_to_add.title: Implement WorkerRuntimeSelectionService
tasks_to_add.problem: Hub needs deterministic selection logic that chooses a worker and runtime target from policy, capabilities, environment, safety class and current health.
implementation_notes: WorkerCandidate, RejectedWorkerRuntimeCandidate and WorkerRuntimeSelectionDecision added
WorkerRuntimeSelectionService added in agent/services/worker_runtime_selection_service.py
Fixed selection fails closed when worker unavailable or invalid
Automatic/policy-ranked selection ranks deterministic candidates
Rejected candidates include reason codes and missing capabilities
Cloud runtime rejected when allow_cloud=false
External worker/runtime rejected when allow_external_workers=false
Hermes/ShellGPT rejected for mutation capabilities
OpenCode preferred for patch_propose capability
Native Ananta Worker preferred for repair.execute capabilities
Deterministic same-input selection covered by tests
tests added in tests/test_worker_runtime_selection_service.py
acceptance_criteria: Service returns selected_worker_id, selected_worker_kind, selected_runtime_target_id, selected_runtime_kind, selection_mode, decision_status, selected_reason, rejected_candidates, required_capabilities, missing_capabilities, policy_decision_ref and context_boundary_decision.
Fixed worker selection returns denied if fixed worker is unavailable or lacks capabilities; it does not silently auto-fallback unless fallback_policy explicitly allows it.
Automatic selection ranks candidates by safety, capability match, context boundary, runtime health, locality, risk profile and configured priority.
OpenCode is selected only for task modes/capabilities it supports and never for unsupported deterministic repair mutation unless policy explicitly maps it to safe proposal/command plan mode.
Hermes/external worker is rejected for mutation execution by default and may only be selected for analysis/preview/proposal if context policy allows.
Native Ananta Worker is preferred for deterministic RepairProcedureRunner execution when it has required tools and runtime target.
Selection decision is deterministic for the same inputs.
test_requirements: Fixed native worker selection positive test.
Fixed unavailable worker denial test.
Automatic native-vs-opencode-vs-hermes ranking test.
Cloud/external worker rejected when allow_cloud=false test.
Determinism test with same input ordering.
anti_patterns: Do not use random/LLM-based worker selection.
Do not select a worker that cannot run the required runtime target.
Do not hide rejected candidates.

tasks_to_add.title: Persistenced selected worker and runtime target in repair plan/job/result
tasks_to_add.problem: After execution it must be clear which worker and runtime actually ran the repair, not only which backend was preferred.
implementation_notes: SelectedWorkerRuntimeRef added in worker/core/runtime_target.py
SelectedWorkerRuntimeRef.from_decision() converts selected decisions into persistable metadata
WorkerJobDB and WorkerResultDB fields added in agent/db_models.py
WorkerJobService.create_job and complete_job updated to persist selection data
acceptance_criteria: RepairProcedureExecutionPlan includes worker_selection_policy and optional preselected_worker_runtime_decision_ref.
WorkerJobDB stores selected_worker_id, selected_worker_kind, selected_runtime_target_id, selected_runtime_kind, selection_mode and selection_decision_ref or equivalent metadata.
ExecutionEnvelope includes effective selected worker/runtime metadata.
RepairExecutionResult includes actual_worker_id, actual_worker_kind, actual_runtime_target_id, actual_runtime_kind, selection_reason and rejected_candidates_ref.
RepairOutcomeDB stores selected worker/runtime metadata for later analysis.
Tests prove selected worker/runtime survive plan -> job -> result -> outcome persistence.
test_requirements: Persistence roundtrip test.
Mismatch detection test if actual runtime differs from planned selection.
anti_patterns: Do not only store preferred_backend.
Do not lose runtime target after WorkerResult is persisted.

tasks_to_add.title: Add API for worker/runtime candidates and selection preview
tasks_to_add.problem: UI needs an API to show available workers, runtime targets, automatic decision preview and rejected candidates before repair execution.
implementation_notes: Endpoints should be read-only for preview unless explicit update/create action is called.
Expose enough details for UI, but no tokens/secrets.
Endpoints /repair/candidates and /repair/runtime-targets added to agent/routes/repair.py
Preview selection logic integrated into /repair/preview
acceptance_criteria: API exposes list worker candidates with worker_id, kind, display_name, capabilities, roles, health_state, validation_errors, supported_execution_modes and risk flags.

API exposes list runtime targets with runtime_target_id, runtime_kind, display_name, health_state, workspace_scope_summary, allowed_capabilities, denied_capabilities, data_boundary and resource limits.
API exposes preview endpoint that accepts WorkerSelectionPolicy + repair plan/task context and returns WorkerRuntimeSelectionDecision without executing repair.
Preview response includes selected candidate, rejected candidates with reason codes, cloud/context boundary warnings and approval implications.
API redacts URLs/tokens/secrets according to operator permissions.
Tests cover candidate list, preview fixed selection, preview automatic selection and redaction.
test_requirements: API tests for GET /workers/candidates or project-equivalent endpoint.
API tests for runtime target list.
API tests for POST /repair/selection-preview or equivalent.
anti_patterns: Do not make UI guess candidates from raw agent rows.
Do not expose agent tokens in candidate API.
Do not execute repair from preview endpoint.

tasks_to_add.title: Add Angular UI for fixed worker and runtime assignment
tasks_to_add.problem: Operators need to explicitly pin a repair/task/blueprint role to a worker such as OpenCode, native Ananta Worker or Hermes, and to a concrete runtime target such as local Docker, WSL or remote worker.
implementation_notes: Locate actual Angular frontend path before implementing.
Prefer a reusable WorkerSelectionComponent used by repair preview, blueprint role editor and task creation if practical.
Use constrained selects, not raw JSON first. Advanced JSON may be secondary.
acceptance_criteria: UI offers selection mode: Automatic, Fixed Worker, Policy Ranked.
Fixed Worker mode displays worker kind options including native Ananta Worker, OpenCode, Hermes and registered remote/custom workers from API.
Fixed Worker mode displays only compatible runtime targets for the selected worker or clearly marks incompatible targets disabled with reason.
Operator can select a concrete runtime target such as local Docker/Compose, WSL, remote HTTP worker or cloud worker if available and permitted.
UI shows health/validation state for selected worker/runtime before saving or executing.
UI blocks save/execute when selected worker/runtime violates required capability or cloud/context policy unless user has explicit override/approval flow.
Saving does not drop existing advanced fields like capabilities, worker_roles, execution_limits or BlueprintRoleDB.config.
test_requirements: Frontend unit/component test for fixed worker selection.
Frontend test that incompatible runtime target is disabled with reason.
Frontend test that save payload contains worker_selection and runtime_target fields.
anti_patterns: Do not expose only a free-text preferred_backend field.
Do not silently overwrite advanced config fields when saving from UI.
Do not show cloud worker as equivalent to local worker without warning.

tasks_to_add.title: Add Angular UI for automatic worker/runtime policy configuration
tasks_to_add.problem: Automatic selection must be understandable and configurable: prefer local, allow cloud, allow external workers, require deterministic runner, prefer OpenCode for patch proposal, prefer native worker for execution, etc.
implementation_notes: Policy editor should use safe presets plus advanced options.
Presets: Strict Local, Balanced Local First, Proposal External Allowed, Cloud Allowed With Approval.
acceptance_criteria: UI provides safe presets for automatic selection: Strict Local, Local First, External Analysis Only, Cloud Allowed With Approval or equivalent.
UI allows toggling prefer_local, allow_cloud, allow_external_workers, require_deterministic_runner and fallback_policy within validated constraints.
UI allows allowed_worker_kinds and denied_worker_kinds multi-select.
UI displays predicted selection preview and rejected candidate reasons before execution.
UI warns when a preset allows cloud/external worker and context may be sensitive.
UI persists policy into BlueprintRoleDB.config, task repair config or relevant API model without dropping fields.
test_requirements: Frontend test for Strict Local preset producing allow_cloud=false and external workers denied.
Frontend test for External Analysis Only preset allowing Hermes for proposal but not mutation execution.
Frontend test for preview rendering selected and rejected candidates.
anti_patterns: Do not make automatic selection a black box.
Do not hide fallback behavior from operator.
Do not enable cloud by default.

tasks_to_add.title: Bind worker/runtime selection into BlueprintRoleDB.config
tasks_to_add.problem: BlueprintRoleDB.config already holds preferred_backend and execution_mode concepts, but it must support full worker/runtime policy so roles can deterministically choose execution backend and runtime environment.
implementation_notes: Legacy preferred_backend normalization exists in WorkerSelectionPolicyService
Keep backward compatibility with preferred_backend by mapping it into WorkerSelectionPolicy when possible.
Validate config on create/update/import.
worker_selection validation added to blueprint role update in agent/routes/teams.py
Validation for worker_selection and runtime_target added to agent/services/blueprint_bundle_service.py
acceptance_criteria: BlueprintRoleDB.config supports worker_selection and runtime_target_policy or equivalent nested fields.
preferred_backend remains supported as legacy shorthand but is expanded/normalized to WorkerSelectionPolicy in read model.
Config validation rejects impossible combinations such as fixed Hermes worker for mutation repair execution unless policy explicitly permits analysis-only mode.
Bundle export/import preserves worker_selection and runtime_target_policy fields.
Role editor/API roundtrip preserves existing capability_defaults, risk_profile and verification_defaults together with worker/runtime config.
Tests cover create/update/read/export/import roundtrip.
test_requirements: Backend role config API tests.
Bundle roundtrip tests.
Legacy preferred_backend normalization test.
anti_patterns: Do not keep only preferred_backend once runtime target matters.
Do not silently drop worker_selection fields during blueprint save/import.

tasks_to_add.title: Bind worker/runtime selection into Agent registration and validation
tasks_to_add.problem: Agent registration currently may expose only name/url/token/role in UI. The backend must model and validate capabilities, worker_roles, execution_limits, runtime_targets and registration validation state.
implementation_notes: AgentInfoDB already has worker_roles, capabilities, execution_limits, registration_validated and validation_errors fields
Show validation_errors read-only in UI.
Capabilities and roles must be registry-validated, not arbitrary strings.
runtime_targets field added to AgentInfoDB
AgentRegisterRequest and AgentDirectoryEntryContract updated with runtime_targets
AgentRegistryService.validate_registration_payload and build_registered_agent updated to handle runtime_targets
acceptance_criteria: Agent registration model/API supports capabilities, worker_roles, execution_limits, runtime_targets, registration_validated and validation_errors.
UI displays and edits capabilities and worker_roles via validated controls or advanced validated editor.
UI displays and edits execution_limits such as max_parallel_tasks with min/max validation.
UI displays runtime targets per agent and their health/validation state.
registration_validated and validation_errors are visible read-only unless an explicit revalidate action is used.
Invalid capability/role/runtime target is rejected by API and shown in UI.
Existing agent edit flow no longer drops capabilities, worker_roles, execution_limits or validation fields.
test_requirements: Agent API roundtrip test for capabilities, worker_roles and execution_limits.
Agent runtime target validation test.
Frontend test ensuring edit/save preserves advanced fields.
anti_patterns: Do not let UI save only name/url/token/role and wipe advanced agent fields.
Do not make validation_errors user-editable.
Do not accept unregistered capabilities silently.

tasks_to_add.title: Record worker/runtime selection in preview, trace, audit and operator view
tasks_to_add.problem: Operators must be able to see not only what repair will do, but which worker/runtime will do it and why.
implementation_notes: SelectedWorkerRuntimeRef contract exists
WorkerRuntimeSelectionDecision includes decision_hash, selected reason and rejected candidates
Add selection info to preview before execution, and actual info after execution.
Rejected candidates should be summarized in UI; full list can be detail panel.
Audit event 'worker_runtime_selected_preview' added to /repair/preview
TraceBundleV2 extended with worker_runtime_selection field
acceptance_criteria: Repair preview shows selected worker/runtime or fixed assignment and rejected candidates with reason codes.
RepairExecutionResult includes selected_worker_id, selected_worker_kind, selected_runtime_target_id, selected_runtime_kind and selection_decision_ref.
TraceBundle includes worker_runtime_selection with selected and rejected candidates refs/hashes.
Audit emits worker_runtime_selected before execution and includes selection_mode, worker_kind, runtime_kind and policy_decision_ref.
Operator view clearly shows automatic vs fixed assignment and whether fallback was used.
Tests prove preview and final execution contain matching selection refs unless fallback policy explicitly changes them.
test_requirements: Preview selection API test.
Trace/audit selection event test.
E2E test verifying actual selected runtime recorded after execution.
anti_patterns: Do not make worker/runtime selection invisible in execution history.
Do not hide fallback worker use.

tasks_to_add.title: Add E2E tests for fixed and automatic worker/runtime selection
tasks_to_add.problem: The final proof must show that UI/API policy leads to the expected worker and runtime target during real deterministic repair flow.
implementation_notes: Unit tests cover fixed native worker selection
Unit tests cover fixed unavailable worker denial
Unit tests cover automatic native-vs-opencode-vs-hermes selection
Unit tests cover cloud/external rejection
Unit tests cover deterministic selection
Use fake registered workers/runtimes: native local Docker, OpenCode local, Hermes external analysis-only, cloud worker blocked.
No real external workers or cloud calls.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed native worker + local Docker runtime repair preview selects that exact worker/runtime.
Fixed unavailable OpenCode worker returns denied/no_eligible_worker and does not fallback silently.
Automatic Strict Local policy selects native/local runtime and rejects Hermes/cloud worker with clear reasons.
Automatic External Analysis Only policy may select Hermes for analysis/preview but rejects Hermes for mutation execution.
Automatic policy with OpenCode preferred for patch proposal selects OpenCode only for proposal mode and records runtime target.
Cloud worker candidate is rejected when allow_cloud=false before context is sent.
Actual execution result and RepairOutcomeDB record the selected worker/runtime.
test_requirements: pytest tests/test_repair_runtime_worker_selection_e2e.py
No external network/API key required.
anti_patterns: Do not test only selection service without full repair path.
Do not allow cloud candidate to receive context during denied-selection test.

definition_of_done_global_to_add: WorkerSelectionPolicy supports fixed, automatic and policy-ranked selection for native Ananta Worker, OpenCode, Hermes and registered remote/custom workers.
WorkerRuntimeTarget models where the worker actually runs: local process, Docker/Compose, WSL, remote worker, CI sandbox or cloud runtime.
Hub validates worker/runtime selection before repair execution and records rejected candidates with reasons.
Angular UI allows fixed worker/runtime assignment and automatic selection policy configuration without dropping advanced agent/blueprint fields.
Preview shows selected worker/runtime before execution; result/outcome/trace/audit show actual selected worker/runtime after execution.
Fixed assignment fails closed when the selected worker/runtime cannot satisfy capabilities, health, context, cloud or safety policy.
Automatic selection does not silently choose cloud/external workers when allow_cloud=false or context is sensitive.
E2E tests prove fixed assignment and automatic selection behave correctly through the deterministic repair flow.
